title: search-service 500s immediately after v2.4.0 deploy
summary: Immediately after the v2.4.0 rollout at 16:40 UTC, search-service began throwing 500s isolated to the newly added /recommendations endpoint. Other endpoints (e.g. /search) are healthy and traffic is flat.

ground_truth.root_cause: v2.4.0 added a /recommendations endpoint that reads user_profile.preferences.get(...) without a null check. For users who have no saved preferences, preferences is None, raising AttributeError ('NoneType' object has no attribute 'get') and returning 500. The bug shipped in the new release: error rate steps from 0% to ~87% exactly at deploy time and is confined to the new endpoint.
key_evidence: search-service log: 'deploy v2.4.0 complete (commit a1b2c3d)' at 16:40, immediately followed by 500s
search-service log: "AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get' at recommendations.py:54"
errors occur only on GET /recommendations (the new endpoint); GET /search is unaffected
expected_fix_themes: add a null/default guard for users without preferences (or backfill default preferences)
roll back v2.4.0 or ship a forward hotfix to the recommendations handler
add a regression test for the no-preferences case and gate new endpoints behind a feature flag
